description: Automatically ban IP addresses based on failed login attempts. Monitors Windows Event Viewer and log files. Free and open-source.
notes: IPBan runs as a Windows service and requires administrator privileges.
post_install: if (-not ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal][Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)) { Write-Warning 'IPBan requires administrator privileges. Reinstall with: scoop install -g ipban'; return }
if ($svc) { if ($svc.Status -ne 'Stopped') { Stop-Service -Name 'IPBan' -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Start-Sleep -Seconds 2 }; sc.exe delete 'IPBan' | Out-Null; Start-Sleep -Seconds 2 }
Write-Host 'IPBan service registered. Run Start-Service IPBan to start it.' -ForegroundColor Green
pre_uninstall: if ($svc) { if ($svc.Status -ne 'Stopped') { Stop-Service -Name 'IPBan' -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Start-Sleep -Seconds 2 }; sc.exe delete 'IPBan' | Out-Null }
